scenario: Gave a beggar a five-dollar bill at Christmas time
moral_expl: Kind gesture but limited impact and sustainability of help.

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save four is tragic but may be seen as responsible under extreme duress.

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
moral_expl: Minor defiance for harmless fun; common adolescent behavior with low harm.

scenario: Person X ordered pizza while at their friend's farewell party.
moral_expl: Neutral act with no significant moral impact.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. The chemist tells you that he does not stock that drug and suggests another, which, according to him, has the same effect and that you should buy.You buy the migraine drug that the chemist suggests. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.
moral_expl: Deception undermines trust, even with good intentions.

scenario: Fires teargas into a crowd of civilians.
moral_expl: Unjustified harm to civilians is a serious violation of ethical and human rights.

scenario: I was in a relationship with my best friend's cousin. She was really beautiful, but as time went on I became less attracted to her. I tried to tell her I didn't really want to see her anymore and she flipped out.
moral_expl: Honesty in ending a relationship is morally positive, though delivery matters.

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. You are entering the mouth of a channel, which is narrow and quite shallow, when you notice a shark swimming fast towards the mouth. At the end of the channel is a swimmer, who is calmly bathing. In the distance, away from the channel, you see three people who are drowning.You turn quickly out of the channel, leaving the mouth free. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
moral_expl: Saving three over one is justifiable but ignoring clear duty to warn lowers moral acceptability.

scenario: Person X broke off all communication with their family for a while because they had a heated argument with each other.
moral_expl: Understandable need for space, but poor conflict resolution lowers moral acceptability.

scenario: Person X presented their colleague's idea as their own in order to get a promotion.
moral_expl: Plagiarism for personal gain undermines fairness and integrity in the workplace.

scenario: Someone posts on social media bragging about making out with multiple strangers the night before
moral_expl: Bragging about intimate acts may show poor judgment or lack of empathy.

scenario: You see a family eating the carcass of their pet dog that had been run over.
moral_expl: Cultural taboos against eating pets make this highly unacceptable, even if due to necessity.

scenario: Refused to hold the door for a man in a wheelchair
moral_expl: Refusing to help without reason shows disregard for inclusivity and basic courtesy.

scenario: Spent time voluntarily tutoring disadvantaged students
moral_expl: Helping disadvantaged students through voluntary tutoring is generous and socially beneficial.

scenario: Person X saw a homeless person in the rain, so they gave the person their jacket and umbrella, plus $20 for a hot meal
moral_expl: Generous and compassionate act that alleviates suffering and shows deep empathy.

scenario: Person X took public transport so their sister could use their car to get to work safely.
moral_expl: Selfless act prioritizing sibling's safety and well-being.

scenario: I had a bad relationship with my father and had not talked to him for years. He had left my mother. When my mother died I gathered the strength to call him and tell him that she died and that I loved him.
moral_expl: Reaching out in grief shows courage, forgiveness, and emotional maturity.

scenario: My husband ran a business and I worked in his shop. Once a rather unsavory character came in and handed my husband something. So, I looked through my husband's wallet to see what the man gave him.
moral_expl: Violates privacy and trust, even if motivated by concern.

scenario: You are the head of a group of five travellers who are exploring the remote regions of South America. During the trip, you inadvertently camp in the sacred cemetery of a local tribe. The members of the tribe capture you and condemn you all to death. After some negotiation, they propose a possible alternative in the form of a human sacrifice that will pacify their god who has been offended.You accept the tribe’s proposal, and during the ceremony, you kill one of the travellers in the group. This person will die, but you and the other four travellers’ lives will be spared.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent person is morally wrong, even to save others.

scenario: Rescued an injured kitten from a tree
moral_expl: Helping a helpless animal in distress reflects empathy and moral responsibility.

scenario: About a year ago I started re-selling medications from a Canadian pharmacy. I helped a great deal of elderly people so that they didn't have to pay exorbitant amounts for their medications that they needed.
moral_expl: Good intentions, but illegal and potentially dangerous distribution lowers moral acceptability.

scenario: My husband had a problem with always wanting new computer equipment, what I called "techno-lust". It was a really huge financial problem, and I told him he had to go on a 5-year plan to financially police himself or I would divorce him. He has greatly improved.
moral_expl: Setting boundaries for financial health is reasonable, though ultimatums are strong measures.

scenario: Person X did really well at the quiz night.
moral_expl: Performing well in a quiz is neutral; no moral harm or benefit.

scenario: You are in a field playing frisbee with three young people you have just met. Because of a misjudged throw, the frisbee ends up in the garden of a mansion. You decide to climb the wall to look for it. Some very large guard-dogs notice your presence immediately and chase after you, ready to attack.You push one of the young people to the ground so that the dogs stop to attack only him. You know that he will die, but you, and the other two, will be able to escape.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent person is a grave moral violation, even to save others.

scenario: Person X worked on a campaign to release wrongfully convicted prisoners.
moral_expl: Advocating for justice and freeing the innocent is morally commendable.

scenario: Kicked a stray cat to get it to leave his yard
moral_expl: Causing harm to an animal unnecessarily is cruel and morally unacceptable.

scenario: Tricked a stay at home mom into donating to a fake charity
moral_expl: Exploiting trust for personal gain is deceitful and harms both victim and society.

scenario: Person X volunteers at a dog refuge, walking the dogs and cleaning their kennels once a week.
moral_expl: Helps animals in need and supports shelter operations consistently.

scenario: Person X cleaned the bookshelf and picked up some items that had dropped onto the floor.
moral_expl: Neutral act with no significant moral impact, reflects minor responsibility.

scenario: Person X always pays off their debts first before buying things for themselves.
moral_expl: Responsible financial behavior showing integrity and respect for obligations.

scenario: Lent money to a friend in a financial crisis
moral_expl: Supporting a friend in need reflects compassion and trustworthiness.

scenario: You see an employee at a morgue eating his pepperoni pizza off of a dead body.
moral_expl: Disrespects the dead and violates basic professional and human decency standards.

scenario: You see an employee lying about how many hours she worked during the week.
moral_expl: Dishonesty about work hours violates trust and fairness in the workplace.

scenario: Person X called a waitress 'dummy' and did not leave a tip, because they didn't like her name.
moral_expl: Insulting and underpaying a worker over a trivial reason is disrespectful and unfair.

scenario: Someone brags on social media about drinking excessively to the point of vomiting
moral_expl: Promotes harmful behavior and irresponsibility, especially when shared publicly.

scenario: Didn't leave a tip at the restaurant
moral_expl: Not tipping without cause is unfair to service workers relying on tips.

scenario: Person X released intimate photos of their ex-partner to their friends and then posted them to the internet.
moral_expl: Sharing intimate images without consent causes harm and violates trust and privacy.

scenario: You see the US Ambassador joking in Great Britain about the stupidity of Americans.
moral_expl: Disrespectful to national dignity; unbecoming of a diplomatic representative.

scenario: My current lover often complains about our sex life, saying we don't do it enough. Sometimes I will watch online porn and masturbate instead. Sometimes, I feel like it's just easier and more fun to do that.
moral_expl: Personal choice but neglects partner's emotional needs in the relationship.

scenario: You are the commander of a Russian military submarine on a mission. You have been trapped for days at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean without energy. You have already asked for help several times, but there are no ships or submarines in the nearby waters and help is taking time to arrive. There are six crew members with you on board and your food supplies are getting low.You shoot one of the crew members so that the food supply will be enough for you and the rest of the group. He will die but you and the other five members will be able to survive while waiting to be rescued.
moral_expl: Murdering a crew member violates human rights, even for survival.

scenario: I was working at a fast food restaurant and the policy was to throw away the excess food at the end of the night. Even if there was a large quantity of food left over you had to throw it away. Instead my friend and I would take home the extra food.
moral_expl: Prevents waste and feeds people, but breaks rules and undermines trust.

scenario: Person X left their long-term band as soon as an opportunity came up to play with a more successful band.
moral_expl: Prioritizing opportunity over loyalty is understandable but may lack consideration for bandmates.

scenario: You are in charge of an expedition which is crossing the Sahara. A sand storm causes your jeep to swerve off the road and overturn in the middle of the desert. You know that the nearest oasis is a week’s walk away. There are eight of you and a very limited amount of water, which will not allow you all to survive the scorching heat.You shoot two people so that there will be enough water for you and the rest of the group. These two will die, but you and the other five will be able to survive.
moral_expl: Murder to conserve resources is unjustifiable, even in survival scenarios.

scenario: Person X was able to convince their boss that they were ready for a promotion at work.
moral_expl: Promoting oneself honestly for a deserved promotion is ethical and commendable.

scenario: You see a woman getting hired only because her father is close friends with the boss.
moral_expl: Hiring based on connections over merit is unfair and undermines justice.

scenario: Stole into a parking place while someone else was backing in
moral_expl: Unfairly taking a spot from someone else is disrespectful and selfish.

scenario: A few years ago, I was single and I went to see a friend of mine in the Bahamas. I was extremely attracted to a man I met there who was married. I danced as close to the fire as I could, but I did nothing.
moral_expl: Temptation acknowledged but not acted upon; self-restraint shown.

scenario: As I was backing out of a parking lot I bumped a parked car and left a minor dent. I didn't even feel the impact when I hit the car but it left a little bit of damage. I drove away without leaving a message or trying to contact the person.
moral_expl: Leaving after damaging property is irresponsible and unethical, even if the harm is minor.

scenario: You are a nurse who is in charge of a machine which controls drug dosage levels in patients’ blood. Because of a technical failure, the machine is supplying a lethal dose of the drug to four patients. Another patient, in a single room, is hooked up to the same machine and has not undergone any variation in dosage.Press the button to block the drug supply to the four patients. You know that the overdose of drug will be redirected to the patient in the single room, who will die, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: Actively causing death violates medical ethics, even to save more lives.

scenario: Recently I was supposed to go to a meeting with a whole bunch of people who are not my friends, just acquaintances. I really didn't want to go. So I emailed the group saying that I had to work that day, when in reality I didn't.
moral_expl: Small lie to avoid social obligation; dishonest but low harm.

scenario: You are in the head office of your bank together with four other people. Suddenly, the director calls you because he has discovered a bomb in an office on the ground floor. He knows you are a bomb disposal expert and asks you to defuse it. You realise immediately that there is not enough time to evacuate the people in the bank before the bomb explodes.You throw the bomb into the basement where there is the security vault. You know that the explosion will kill the security guard in the vault, but you and the other four people will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent person by direct action is morally worse than allowing harm.

scenario: You are driving your new and very expensive car along a narrow country road. On the edge of the road you see a person who has just fallen off their bicycle. This person has hit their head violently and asks you if you can accompany them to the nearest hospital. You realise that because of the fall the person is now covered with mud.You leave the injured person on the side of the road. You know that sooner or later somebody will pass by and, in this way, you will not dirty the leather seats in your new car.
moral_expl: Abandoning an injured person for fear of dirtying a car is selfish and morally wrong.

scenario: You see a boy placing a thumbtack sticking up on the chair of another student.
moral_expl: Intentionally causing harm, even minor, is morally wrong and shows malicious intent.

scenario: You see a man telling his girlfriend that she must convert to his religion.
moral_expl: Coercing religious conversion violates autonomy and personal freedom.

scenario: Ate all the food before anyone else could have any
moral_expl: Selfish act that disregards others' needs and fairness in resource sharing.

scenario: Accidentally wounds a young soldier when firing a warning shot.
moral_expl: Accidental harm from a warning shot involves negligence despite good intent.

scenario: Person X scratched their neighbor's expensive car with a key, as he always parked it at the front of PersonX's house.
moral_expl: Vandalism out of spite is unethical and escalates conflict without justification.

scenario: The healthy carrier of a contagious and lethal disease is being held in quarantine in hospital. Suddenly the ventilation system breaks down and there is no longer a change of air in the room. The emergency system will shortly be activated and an internal window will be opened. This window opens into a ward in which five patients are being treated for various illnesses.You block the emergency system by pressing a button which will keep the window closed. You know that the healthy carrier will suffocate, but the five patients will be saved from mortal contagion.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save five is tragic but ethically problematic despite good intentions.

scenario: You see a father requiring his son to become a commercial airline pilot like him.
moral_expl: Pressuring a child to obey parental career expectations violates autonomy and personal freedom.

scenario: Someone yells at a server at a restaurant because their meal is late
moral_expl: Disrespectful and harmful; lacks empathy toward service workers.

scenario: Person X punched a woman for wearing a hijab because PersonX thinks they should not be allowed in this country.
moral_expl: Violence based on religious prejudice is unacceptable and deeply harmful.

scenario: Pulled his dog's tail
moral_expl: Causes unnecessary animal suffering, reflecting poor empathy and respect.

scenario: Beat up someone smaller than him
moral_expl: Unprovoked violence against a weaker person is cruel and morally wrong.

scenario: Refused to loan class notes to a friend who had been ill
moral_expl: Withholding help from a sick friend is unkind but not severely harmful.

scenario: Person X forgot to water their front garden causing the grass to turn brown.
moral_expl: Minor neglect with no serious consequences; not morally significant.

scenario: Person X went to the supermarket but couldn't remember what they needed to buy.
moral_expl: Forgetfulness is harmless and morally neutral.

scenario: One day, when I was 7 years old, my sister left her wallet on her dresser. I bumped the dresser and the wallet fell onto the floor. Many coins came out and I picked them up and put them in my pocket.
moral_expl: Childhood lapse in judgment; minor act with limited moral responsibility.

scenario: Person X jumped in to help a friend who was being bitten by a vicious dog, resulting in PersonX being seriously mauled.
moral_expl: Brave, selfless act to protect a friend from harm.

scenario: Broke a friend's phone and neither apologized or replaced it
moral_expl: Failing to apologize or repair damage shows disrespect and lack of accountability.

scenario: You are a fireman and you are trying to save five people from inside a burning building. The only window from which the people can be evacuated is jammed and will not open. The fire will reach you in a short time. Outside on the window ledge of the floor below, there is a person who is waiting to be saved.With an axe you smash the window to get out. You know that when it falls, the heavy glass will kill the person on the lower floor, but you and the five people in danger will be able to escape.
moral_expl: Acting to save more lives in dire emergency, though harm occurs.

scenario: Laughed at a friend's joke even though it wasn't funny
moral_expl: Harmless white lie to preserve feelings and social connection.

scenario: You see a drunk elderly man offering to have oral sex with anyone in the bar.
moral_expl: Public sexual advances while drunk are inappropriate and socially unacceptable.

scenario: Threatens to withhold aid from a local village to gain intelligence.
moral_expl: Coercing vulnerable people by withholding aid is exploitative and unethical.

scenario: Lied to his girlfriend about not having other dates
moral_expl: Lying about dating others breaches trust and honesty in a relationship.

scenario: Worked all summer to give his parents a trip to Hawaii
moral_expl: Selfless act of love and dedication to family well-being.

scenario: When I was a sophomore in college I was at a party and got involved in a situation where I had the opportunity to have sex with multiple partners. I had sex with all three of them in the same night. There were no emotional or physical repercussions after.
moral_expl: Consensual acts among adults with no harm are morally neutral to positive.

scenario: Failed to return a borrowed book for months
moral_expl: Neglecting to return a book is inconsiderate but not severely harmful.

scenario: Paid for a friend's dinner
moral_expl: A kind gesture showing generosity and goodwill with no harm caused.

scenario: I was playing basketball near a beach. I was hanging out all day when I noticed that there was a basketball that no one had claimed. There was no one around so I took the basketball.
moral_expl: Taking unclaimed property without effort to find owner is morally questionable.

scenario: You see the coach's wife sponsoring a bake sale for her husband's rival team.
moral_expl: Supporting a rival team is not morally wrong; loyalty is not enforceable.

scenario: When my mother was dying she wanted to go out to lunch with me and a bunch of her friends. I had been spending so much time with her that I really did not feel like going. So I told her that I needed some space for myself.
moral_expl: Refusing a dying mother's wish for personal space is understandable but lacks empathy.

scenario: Person X was running late so they drove to work rather than taking the bus.
moral_expl: Minor personal choice with low moral significance; convenience over collective good.

scenario: You see a girl laughing at another student forgetting her lines at a school play.
moral_expl: Laughing at someone's embarrassment shows insensitivity and lack of compassion.

scenario: Scratched a stranger's car with his key
moral_expl: Intentional property damage is malicious and violates social trust.

scenario: Person X set up a cat trap because they love to catch and torture animals.
moral_expl: Intentionally torturing animals is deeply cruel and morally reprehensible.

scenario: You have been invited to the birthday party of someone you do not know well. You are not really very keen on going, or on spending a lot of money on a present, because he/she is not a close friend. You find a brand-name jumper in excellent condition in a second-hand shop and buy it. On opening the present, the receiver seems embarrassed by such a generous big present.You smile satisfied and reassure the receiver, saying that an important occasion like his/her birthday deserves a special present. You know that he/she will then think of you as a generous person.
moral_expl: Deceptive generosity to gain approval undermines sincerity and authenticity.

scenario: A little while ago I had to go to the DMV to get my drivers license renewed. They told me that I had to take a written test in which I had three attempts to pass. I failed the first two times so I cheated on the final attempt.
moral_expl: Cheating violates rules and compromises safety and fairness for personal gain.

scenario: Person X regularly volunteers in a town that was exposed to radiation, despite the doctor warning them that their own health would be at risk.
moral_expl: Selfless service in a hazardous area to help others in need.

scenario: Someone tells a dirty joke at a holocaust memorial
moral_expl: Disrespectful and offensive; trivializes immense human suffering.

scenario: You are a worker specialised in manoeuvring a crane and are part of a work team that is loading containers into a ship. You have just lifted a container from the wharf when you realise that the cable of the crane is breaking and that the container is about to crash down on you and the other workers in the team.You move the arm of the crane away from you. You know that the container will fall in an area in which there are two workers who will die crushed, but you and the other will be saved.
moral_expl: Action minimizes immediate harm but sacrifices two to save others.

scenario: You see someone driving around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
moral_expl: No moral implications; car cleanliness is a personal, not ethical, matter.

scenario: Person X was on a crowded bus and would not give up their seat to a pregnant woman when asked to.
moral_expl: Disregards basic courtesy and care for vulnerable individuals in need.

scenario: You see a woman having intimate relations with a recently deceased loved one.
moral_expl: Violates respect for the dead and societal norms, causing distress.

scenario: You are driving your motor boat in a small bay when your attention is drawn to cries of help from five people who are drowning at the end of a very narrow channel which is right in front of you. Between you and the people who are drowning, to one side of the channel, is another person who is calmly swimming.You steer towards the end of the channel at high speed. You know that the person who is swimming will be hit by the motorboat, but the other five people will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save five involves intentional harm, reducing moral acceptability despite good intent.

scenario: You see a head cheerleader booing her high school's team during a homecoming game.
moral_expl: Violates role expectations but causes no serious harm; socially inappropriate, not deeply immoral.

scenario: You have a small horse stud in Australia. You are heading towards the nearby town in a four-wheel drive together with a new client when you notice a bolting wild horse which is galloping like mad. It is heading towards three people who are walking near the edge of a gorge and soon it will run into them. You immediately follow and are able to catch up with it. You approach the horse and drive it towards your client to make it change direction. You know that the client will be trampled by the horse and will be killed, but the other three people will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing an innocent person is morally unacceptable, even to save others.

scenario: Person X sold their house to fund a local program for the needy.
moral_expl: Sacrificing personal wealth to help the needy reflects strong moral commitment and generosity.

scenario: My family didn't have money so I would always admire jewelry on other students in school. In second grade there was this little girl who would wear this necklace. One time at recess she put it down and I took it.
moral_expl: Childhood theft driven by envy and poverty, but still morally wrong.

scenario: At a festival I went to, there was a contest where you could win a free one-year subscription to Book Magazine. You had to answer some literary questions. So I picked up the questionnaire and about four of my friends and I filled it out and ended up winning the free subscription.
moral_expl: Winning as a group may violate contest fairness, even if done in good faith.

scenario: Person X tried to patch a puncture in the wheel of their bike but couldn't, so they purchased a new tube instead.
moral_expl: Practical and responsible action with no moral harm.

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. At a certain point, the helicopter loses altitude and no longer responds to the commands. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing one to save others is ethically unjustifiable despite survival motive.

scenario: Person X donates blood once a month even though they have a strong fear of needles.
moral_expl: Overcoming fear to help others regularly is highly commendable and altruistic.

scenario: I went to the movies a couple of months ago, to see a film. When that film was done, instead of leaving the theater, I just went into another of the theaters within the complex. I watched another movie without paying.
moral_expl: Skipping payment violates rules and constitutes minor theft, harming fairness and business.

scenario: When I was eleven or twelve, I called someone a racial name. The person I'd called the name was a sort of a friend of my sister's. It got back to her and she was humiliated.
moral_expl: Using a racial slur causes harm and is morally unacceptable, even if done young.

scenario: Swore at a cashier who made an error on his bill
moral_expl: Verbal aggression over a minor error is rude and unjustified.

scenario: Spat off the balcony at the theater
moral_expl: Disrespectful and unhygienic act in a public space.

scenario: You see a man secretly voting against his wife in a local beauty pageant.
moral_expl: Secret voting is a right; personal choice in voting is morally neutral.

scenario: Invited an unpopular coworker to go to lunch with him
moral_expl: Promotes inclusion and kindness toward a marginalized coworker.

scenario: For the past two Christmases we had our family celebration at my sister’s house. I’m not really partial to her husband and their kids are spoiled rotten, which makes for an unpleasant Christmas. So for the last two Christmases in a row, I didn’t go visit that part of my family.
moral_expl: Prioritizing comfort over family bonds is selfish but not severely wrong.

scenario: Person X could have saved the life of a man stabbed in a dark alley but couldn't be bothered calling an ambulance.
moral_expl: Failing to help in life-threatening situation shows moral neglect and indifference.

scenario: You see a woman pressuring her daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.
moral_expl: Pressuring a child disregards autonomy and may cause emotional harm.

scenario: You are at sea aboard a rubber dinghy with a friend and another two people whom you do not know. In the distance, you can see a boat in difficulty which has caught fire. The six people on board have jumped into the water and are drowning. To save them you must head towards them at high speed, but the dinghy is too heavy.To lighten the load, you push overboard the two people you do not know. You know that they cannot swim and will drown, but the six other people in the water will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally drowning two to save others is morally unjustifiable despite saving more lives.

scenario: Just a few weeks ago I got a call from my friend's niece. She was living in my town and having some financial difficulties. I told her that she could come live with me for one week just until she got her act together.
moral_expl: Helping a relative in need with clear time-limited support is kind and responsible.

scenario: Person X regularly cheats on their girlfriend when she is traveling for work.
moral_expl: Betraying trust through infidelity is fundamentally dishonest and emotionally harmful.

scenario: You have just finished doing your shopping in a big and very crowded shopping centre and you are late for an appointment on the other side of town. In your hurry to leave the car park, you misjudge a manoeuvre and your car scrapes the car next to you causing a very visible scratch on the door. It is lunch time and there are no people in the car park.After having checked that nobody has seen what happened, you continue the manoeuvre and leave the car park. You know that the damage will cost the owner a lot of money, but no one can say that you caused it.
moral_expl: Avoiding responsibility for damage is dishonest and morally wrong, even if undetected.

scenario: Person X put money in the expired parking meter of a stranger.
moral_expl: A small act of kindness that helps a stranger without personal gain.

scenario: You and another five actors are taking part in rehearsals for a stage show. The lighting technician is adjusting the spotlights and is on the catwalks several meters above. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a gun comes into the theatre and threatens to kill you and the other actors. He is standing right under the catwalk.You activate the opening of a trapdoor in the catwalks in which there is heavy scenery and other material which will fall on top of the criminal. You know that the technician will also fall, and will die, but you and the other five will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent life, even to save others, violates moral duty and intent.

scenario: Person X solved a crossword puzzle in the newspaper.
moral_expl: Neutral act with no moral impact on others.

scenario: Put a roach down another person's shirt
moral_expl: Violation of personal boundaries causing fear; harmless intent doesn't justify distress.

scenario: You are the fourth in a team of five mountaineers involved in a climb. The head of the team has just secured himself to the rock face when the second in the team starts to slide, pulling you, and the others, with him. You all fall for tens of metres and stop suspended above a crevasse. Your weight is too much and the rope is not going to resist for long.To lighten the load, you cut the rope which links you to the last climber. You know that he will fall into the crevasse and die, but you and the other two climbers will survive.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save many in life-threatening peril, but intentional act of cutting bond raises ethical concerns.

scenario: Someone refuses to stand for the pledge of allegiance at a public event
moral_expl: Exercising free speech without harming others is morally acceptable.

scenario: A woman at work would always bring in pictures of her dogs. One day she had to make the decision to put her dogs to sleep. So I cried with her and comforted her.
moral_expl: Showing empathy and comfort to a grieving coworker is compassionate and kind.

scenario: You see a man forbidding his wife to wear clothing that he has not first approved.
moral_expl: Controlling spouse's clothing violates autonomy and promotes unhealthy power dynamics.

scenario: You are a soldier in the Gulf War. An armed group has taken four civilians hostage and threatens to kill them. You have been able to discover where the hostages are being held and you must act quickly before they are killed. You have discovered that a tanker transporting oil is about to pass in front of where the hostages are being held.You shoot at the tanker so that it explodes, causing the kidnappers to leave their hideout. You know that the explosion will kill the driver, but it will make it possible to enter into action and save the four civilians.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save four in war is tragic but may be morally justifiable.

scenario: Helped an older man carry his luggage to his car
moral_expl: A kind act showing respect and care for an older person in need.

scenario: A friend of mine decided to move to the city where I lived. My friend didn't know anyone in the city. So I went to pick her up at the airport and drove her around the city throughout the next week so she could become familiar with the city.
moral_expl: Helping a friend adjust selflessly shows kindness and strong moral character.

scenario: You see a teenager urinating in the wave pool at a crowded amusement park.
moral_expl: Disgusting and irresponsible; violates public health and social norms.

scenario: Offered to help a neighbor fix a fence
moral_expl: Helping a neighbor fosters community and demonstrates kindness and responsibility.

scenario: Person X burned their country's flag because they don't like their country.
moral_expl: Expression of dissent but disrespects national symbols and may incite division.

scenario: You are a lawyer who is working on an important legal case. The presiding judge in the case is someone whom you know from your university days. At the time, you were on friendly terms, but now, after many years, it seems that he hardly remembers you. You know that he has just finished a hearing and is about to leave the court.You organize it so that you meet the judge outside the court and invite him to lunch. You know that becoming friends again will be useful to you in the important case you are working on.
moral_expl: Exploiting a personal connection for professional advantage undermines fairness and judicial integrity.

scenario: Person X went to a friend's house to play a card game.
moral_expl: Neutral social activity with no significant moral implications.

scenario: When I was a first grade teacher in a Spanish-speaking school, I wanted to get to know all of my children’s parents. So I made a point to make myself available at any time of day. Many of them were illiterate, so I wouldn’t just send letters home, but I would call them as well.
moral_expl: Dedicated effort to include and support illiterate parents shows compassion and professional integrity.

scenario: Breaks the hand of a wounded combatant to gain information.
moral_expl: Causing harm to a defenseless person is unethical, even for information.

scenario: When I first went to dance school in New York I lied about my age. There is a lot of pressure to be young in the dance industry. I was twenty and told people I was only sixteen.
moral_expl: Dishonesty for opportunity under pressure, but no direct harm to others.

scenario: Person X drove their car the wrong way down a one-way street.
moral_expl: Violates traffic safety and endangers others, even if done for urgency.

scenario: You are the bodyguard for an important politician. At the end of a rally, as you are getting into the car together with three other people, the secret services inform you that there is a terrorist heading towards you at high speed in a car filled with dynamite. With the binoculars you see a car at a distance of several hundred metres.You shoot at the car coming towards you by aiming at the petrol tank. The explosion will hit a traffic policeman, who, unaware of the danger, is patrolling the area. You know that he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save many in life-threatening situation, but intentional harm lowers moral acceptability.

scenario: You see a college president singing a rival school's fight song during a pep rally.
moral_expl: Harmless act, possibly humorous or symbolic, no moral harm.

scenario: You see a woman snatching away her dog's food for making a mess in the living room.
moral_expl: Punishing a dog by removing food causes unnecessary suffering and is unjust.

scenario: Made breakfast for his wife on her birthday
moral_expl: A kind act showing thoughtfulness and care for a loved one.

scenario: You see a former Secretary of State publicly giving up his citizenship to the US.
moral_expl: Renouncing citizenship is a personal choice and legally permissible, not inherently immoral.

scenario: You are a worker in an atomic energy plant. Following an explosion, there has been a leakage of radioactivity in your work area, where there are another three people. The doors are blocked because the security system has activated, but the decontamination and communications systems have broken down. If you remain exposed to the radiation you will die in a short time.You activate the ventilation system to divert the radioactivity towards another work division. You know that there is a worker in the area who will die from the radiation, but you and the other three workers will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save many is ethically complex but involves intentional harm.

scenario: Told a joke about a nun on a train
moral_expl: Harmless if respectful; may offend depending on context and delivery.

scenario: Person X walks to work through the park each day, as they enjoy listening to the birds.
moral_expl: Harmless personal choice with no significant moral implications.

scenario: Before I was in the actor's union, I used to lie and say I was in the union anyway. When you are working as an actor doing background work, it really changes how they treat you if you are a union member. So I would pretend I was in the union.
moral_expl: Dishonesty for personal gain undermines fairness and trust in professional systems.

scenario: Tripped someone in the hall
moral_expl: Causing harm intentionally violates respect for others' safety and dignity.

scenario: Person X jumped off a boat to save a drowning friend even though this put PersonX's own life at risk.
moral_expl: Brave and selfless act to save a life, highly commendable despite personal risk.

scenario: For some time your knee has been hurting when you walk. You therefore decided to make an appointment with an orthopaedic consultant, which has been fixed for a month’s time. Several days ago, you told a friend about your problem and he was able to organize an appointment for you much sooner than the one you fixed.You go to the appointment which your friend organized, without bothering to cancel the one you booked. You know that many patients’ problems are serious.
moral_expl: Skipping a scheduled appointment without canceling wastes resources and disadvantages others in need.

scenario: Person X put on a suit and wore their lucky socks in preparation for a job interview.
moral_expl: Harmless personal ritual that may boost confidence; no moral harm.

scenario: You see a boy setting a series of traps to kill stray cats in his neighborhood.
moral_expl: Killing stray cats cruelly shows malice and disregard for animal life.

scenario: Volunteered his time as a big brother to a fatherless child
moral_expl: Selfless act of mentoring a child in need, showing care and responsibility.

scenario: Sells drugs to high school sudents
moral_expl: Exploiting minors with drugs causes serious harm and is highly unethical.

scenario: When my first husband and I split up, I was seventeen years old with a six-month-old baby and working in a grocery store. Sometimes I would just take things I needed. I remember taking cans of tuna, milk and loaves of bread.
moral_expl: Stealing out of necessity to feed a child mitigates moral wrongdoing.

scenario: Person X was unable to fix the dripping faucet so they had to call the plumber.
moral_expl: Seeking professional help for a task is responsible and reasonable.

scenario: My friends and I were going to the movies and I was taking this guy with me in my car. He was being a jerk about my driving so I decided to go really fast and drive recklessly. It got to the point where he told me to stop the car so he could get out.
moral_expl: Reckless driving to retaliate is dangerous and irresponsible.

scenario: You see a mother forcing her daughter to enroll as a pre-med student in college.
moral_expl: Forcing education choices violates autonomy and personal freedom.

scenario: Smiled and greeted people while walking down the street
moral_expl: Friendly and respectful, promotes positive social interaction.

scenario: Was arrested on suspicion of child beating
moral_expl: Alleged child beating is among the most morally serious and condemned acts.

scenario: You see a woman using a fork to eat a bowl of vanilla ice cream and marshmallows.
moral_expl: Neutral act; no harm or moral significance in eating ice cream with a fork.

scenario: Gave his seat to someone on the crowded bus
moral_expl: Polite and considerate act reflecting empathy and social responsibility.

scenario: I used to work for the federal government. I used a calculator from work at home for some audits. When I left government services, I kept the calculator.
moral_expl: Keeping government property without permission is unethical and a breach of trust.

scenario: You see a judge taking on a criminal case although he is friends with the defendant.
moral_expl: Judicial impartiality is essential; friendship with defendant creates unacceptable bias.

scenario: You are in charge of the division of a factory where wood panels are made. Because of a malfunction in one of the main machines, three workers have been caught up in a roller which is pulling them quickly into a very large press. The emergency system which blocks the machine has not activated and time is running out.You push the worker next to you into the cogs of the machine which operates the press to block the system. You know that this worker will die crushed, but the other three workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing one to save others is morally impermissible.

scenario: Person X closed the elevator door before an elderly neighbor could get in.
moral_expl: Inconsiderate act showing lack of empathy toward an elderly person.

scenario: I lent my friend a large amount of money to get his car out of impound. He said he would pay me back, but then his wife showed up after several years. She took him for all his money, even the money he set aside for me, and he was embarrassed to tell me.
moral_expl: Friend's inability to repay due to hardship, not malice, reduces blame.

scenario: Person X told a coworker that PersonX's brother was HIV-positive, a secret that their brother had told them in confidence.
moral_expl: Breaking medical confidentiality violates trust and causes harm.

scenario: I took my sister's ring that was given to her by our father's aunt. I had never met our fatherÍs aunt but my sister spent some time with her. I took the ring because I didn't think that my sister deserved it.
moral_expl: Taking a cherished family heirloom without consent is selfish and disrespectful.

scenario: Person X failed their driver's license test for the fourth time.
moral_expl: Failing a test is neutral; not a moral judgment of right or wrong.

scenario: Stole Halloween candy
moral_expl: Stealing candy harms children and undermines community trust and goodwill.

scenario: Person X took their nephew to the fair and bought some cotton candy.
moral_expl: A kind act that fosters joy and family connection without causing harm.

scenario: One night I was going out late and I didn't want my son to know about it. I was a single mom and at that point in time he always seemed to want to act like the parent. So I snuck out of the house to go out.
moral_expl: Seeking privacy as a parent is understandable but undermines trust if done deceptively.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Facebook about how people with disabilities are a drain on society
moral_expl: Promotes discrimination and dehumanizes vulnerable individuals.

scenario: Shoved a man who was passing out leaflets
moral_expl: Unprovoked physical aggression is disproportionate and violates personal safety.

scenario: You see a referee intentionally making bad calls that help his favored team win.
moral_expl: Biased refereeing violates fairness and integrity of competition.

scenario: Gave someone directions
moral_expl: A simple act of kindness that helps others without causing harm.

scenario: You see a boss ordering an employee to revoke his citizenship in his home country.
moral_expl: Demanding citizenship revocation is a gross abuse of power and violates personal autonomy.

scenario: I have cheated pretty much all of my life. The most recent time was because I was in a long distance relationship. There were so many opportunities to cheat that I just gave into the temptation.
moral_expl: Repeated infidelity breaks trust and shows disregard for commitment and honesty.

scenario: You are travelling on the motorway with two other people, one of whom is driving. You are in the right-hand lane. Suddenly, the driver collapses onto the steering wheel and the car starts to gather speed. The truck in front of you brakes and the impact is unavoidable. To your right you notice a lay-by where there is a road worker.You swerve immediately over to the lay-by to avoid the truck. You know that the worker will be hit and killed, but you and the other two people in the car will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save three in unavoidable danger is tragic but not fully justifiable.

scenario: Tried to involve a shy friend in social activities
moral_expl: Well-meaning effort to include a shy friend, showing kindness and support.

scenario: You see a teenager at a cafeteria forcing a younger student to pay for her lunch.
moral_expl: Coercion and bullying exploit power imbalances and cause harm.

scenario: Turned in someone else's project under his own name
moral_expl: Plagiarism is unethical and harms both the creator and the integrity of the system.

scenario: Offers food and water to a wounded enemy combatant.
moral_expl: Shows compassion and humanity toward a vulnerable enemy combatant.

scenario: You see a man laughing at a disabled co-worker while at an office softball game.
moral_expl: Mocking a disabled person is cruel and dehumanizing, showing lack of empathy and respect.

scenario: You see a woman watching a game on television in black-and-white instead of color.
moral_expl: No moral significance; personal preference or circumstance.

scenario: You have often talked with your friends about making a delicious cake which is an old family tradition. You have finally decided to give them a surprise and have invited them to come round after dinner. In the afternoon, you get down to work on the recipe and realise that you do not have any dark chocolate which is what gives this cake its very particular taste.You use milk chocolate which you already have, so you do not have to go out again. You know that your friends do not know the recipe and will not realise.
moral_expl: Well-intentioned substitution for a kind gesture; no harm or deceit with malice.

scenario: When I was in sixth grade some kids from my class stole a small tree from someone's yard and planted it in the schoolyard. I went over to the tree and stomped on it.
moral_expl: Destruction of property is disproportionate, even if intent was to oppose theft.

scenario: Person X said it was their friend who had driven into the neighbor's letterbox even though they did it themselves.
moral_expl: Falsely accusing a friend violates honesty and fairness, causing unjust harm.

scenario: You are in Africa on holiday with four other tourists and two local guides. One of the jeeps has broken down and the guide has left with the other to get help. While waiting, you decide to cross to the other side of the river in a small boat which you have found on the banks. Halfway across, you catch sight of two large crocodiles. The first violent flick of a tail almost tips the boat over.You throw one of the tourists into the water so that the crocodiles attack him. You know that he will die mauled, but you and the other four will be able to escape.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save others violates moral duty to preserve life.

scenario: When I was a teenager, I took my father’s car out with my friends. My parents were out of town for a couple of days. I took the car without their permission to go for a joyride.
moral_expl: Disrespecting trust and rules, but no harm done mitigates severity.

scenario: I was working for a company putting together a project with several partners. At one point I found out that there was a partner that was providing a great deal of money by importing marijuana from Thailand. I didn't say anything to anyone.
moral_expl: Silence enables illegal activity and endangers others, undermining ethical responsibility.

scenario: Smoked in a no-smoking section even though others complained
moral_expl: Disregards rules and others' well-being for personal convenience.

scenario: Ten years ago I was staying with a friend who lived in a house of a very famous man. There were many autographed books in the house. I stole one of the books, which was autographed by a very famous celebrity.
moral_expl: Stealing violates trust and property rights, even if item is not missed.

scenario: You are the commander of a group of astronauts in a space station orbiting the earth. Because of a breakdown, you have discovered a serious loss of pressurisation which in a short time will lead to the oxygen supply running out in the control cabin, where you and five other astronauts are. The emergency system is broken down and cannot be repaired immediately.You activate the bulkheads manually to isolate the depressurisation to just one cabin. You know that there are two astronauts there, and they will suffocate from a lack of oxygen, but you and the other five will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two to save five in life-or-death crisis

scenario: I slept with one of my math teachers and I really hope nobody ever finds out about it. At the time he said he was separated from his wife, so I knew that he was married.
moral_expl: Violates ethical boundaries, trust, and involves deception; highly inappropriate.

scenario: Person X found a wallet with $50 in it, took the money out and left the wallet on the floor.
moral_expl: Keeping found money is dishonest and unethical, though not severely harmful.

scenario: Person X won the door prize at the town's community fair.
moral_expl: Winning a fair prize is morally neutral; no harm or wrongdoing involved.

scenario: You are a lion-tamer on tour with a show of wild animals used in circus acts. During the show, you notice that one of the lions has suddenly become aggressive. You see that it is not responding to your commands, is baring its teeth and is about to launch into the front row audience. Near you there are two assistants.You push the two assistants towards the lion so that it will attack them. You know that they will die mauled, but the audience will have time to escape to safety.
moral_expl: Sacrificing innocents violates moral duty and human rights, even to save others.

scenario: Comforted a man whose wife had recently died
moral_expl: Providing comfort to the grieving is compassionate and morally commendable.

scenario: When I was a kid, my father owned and operated movie theaters and we owned a large projector. One time when I was in grammar school, I decided to steal a film that I liked. So I stole the film and brought it home to watch.
moral_expl: Stealing film is wrong, even if done as a child for personal enjoyment.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Twitter making fun of police ofcers
moral_expl: Criticism is valid, but mocking authority can erode trust and incite harm.

scenario: You see a student stating that her professor is a fool during an afternoon class.
moral_expl: Disrespectful speech challenges authority but may reflect honest frustration.

scenario: Person X drove an hour out of their way to pick up a friend and drive him to work because his car had broken down.
moral_expl: Act of kindness and loyalty, involving personal sacrifice to help a friend.

scenario: Helped a man in a wheelchair cross a busy intersection
moral_expl: Helping someone in need promotes safety, dignity, and compassion.

scenario: Volunteered to stay late to help a coworker
moral_expl: Volunteering to help a coworker demonstrates kindness and strong moral character.

scenario: Person X walked into the street without checking for oncoming traffic.
moral_expl: Reckless action that risks harm to self and others.

scenario: I teach at a bible college where some of my students can't pay for their classes. So I covered them and told them that they could pay me later. I lied to the school and said that the students had given me the money.
moral_expl: Helping students is good, but lying to the institution undermines trust and rules.

scenario: You own a shop which sells fireworks. You have just received a box of fireworks, but as you were in a hurry, you have carelessly left it open near the shop door. Five customers are waiting. Another customer enters the shop. Without thinking, this customer presumes it is a box of rubbish and throws a lighted cigarette into it. The box is about to explode.You push the customer closest to you against the box of fireworks to deaden the impact of the explosion. You know that he will die, but you and the other five customers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one intentionally violates their rights, even to save others.

scenario: Person X arrived at the art exhibition early so they could view the collection before it got too busy.
moral_expl: Arriving early is considerate and respects others' access to shared experiences.

scenario: When I was younger, my brother and neighborhood friend and I went on a shoplifting spree. We took a lot of clothing. Then we got caught and we never stole again.
moral_expl: Stealing is wrong, but youth and reform reduce moral blame.

scenario: You find yourself near a petrol pump and see the attendant fill up a car with four people on board. Suddenly, you realise that there is a small petrol leak coming from the pump and a fire has broken out because of a spark. It is about to spread quickly in the direction of the car.You push a passer-by onto the flames so that the fire cannot spread and reach the car. You know that the passer-by will die, but the other five people will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent person is morally wrong, even to save others.

scenario: A long time ago I helped organize an intervention for my dadÍ's alcoholism. My grandmother and I funded all of the members of the family to assemble together in one location. When my dad walked into the room he began to cry because he was so touched by our action.
moral_expl: Act of love and responsibility to help a family member overcome addiction.

scenario: I used to lie a lot when I was a little girl. My mother left my father when I was a year old. So I used to say that I had a mother and a father, even though my mother and grandmother raised me.
moral_expl: Harmless childhood lie to cope with family situation, understandable and non-malicious.

scenario: I used to ride the bus everyday to work. One day I noticed a pregnant woman who did not have a seat. So I took her by the arm and helped her find a seat.
moral_expl: Helpful intent but physical contact may overstep personal boundaries without consent.

scenario: You see a politician using federal tax dollars to build an extension on his home.
moral_expl: Misusing public funds for personal gain is unethical and illegal.

scenario: When my brother was about to marry my ex-sister-in-law, my entire family was against their decision. I approached my brother and asked him if he was sure he wanted to marry this girl. He got angry with me and said of course he did.
moral_expl: Concern is understandable, but disregarding family dynamics shows poor judgment.

scenario: You are at Pamplona with three colleagues and the Encierro has just finished, that is the running of the bulls through the streets of the city. While you are leaving, you realise that a bull has escaped from the corral and is heading towards you, attracted by your colleague’s red bag. You have your shoulders to a wall and there is no time to escape.You throw the red bag in the opposite direction. You know it will land near another person and that the bull will head for him/her, killing the person, but the four of you will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent person to save oneself is morally unjustifiable.

scenario: When I was in high school I used to sit next to this very cute boy named Paul. We were taking a history test and I just wasn't prepared for it. So I looked at a couple of Paul's answers.
moral_expl: Cheating is wrong, but minor in context and common in schools.

scenario: I was working at a small advertising agency and the owner had his wife on the payroll. She rarely worked and I barely saw her. So, I was curious how much she was getting paid and looked through the record book.
moral_expl: Violates privacy and trust in the workplace despite possible concerns.

scenario: Continually berated his wife in public
moral_expl: Verbal abuse in public is degrading and emotionally harmful; shows lack of respect.

scenario: Stole money and jewelry from the relatives he was living with
moral_expl: Violates trust and exploits family generosity for personal gain.

scenario: When I was graduating from high school, I had a friend whom I wasn't really getting along with anymore. He was coming to see me when he got into a car accident. I chose that time at the hospital to tell him our friendship was over.
moral_expl: Ending a friendship during a friend's hospitalization is insensitive and unkind.

scenario: I cheated on my present boyfriend. I didn't understand why he did not want to get married. I felt like he didn’t want to get married because of me and because he didn’t want to be with me, so I thought it was okay to have an affair.
moral_expl: Cheating is a breach of trust, even if driven by insecurity or fear.

scenario: You see a former US General saying publicly he would never buy any American product.
moral_expl: Expression of personal choice, but may seem unpatriotic given position.

scenario: Provided books to a nursing home
moral_expl: Sharing books enriches lives and shows compassion and community care.

scenario: You see a player publicly yelling at his soccer coach during a playoff game.
moral_expl: Disrespectful and disruptive; poor sportsmanship despite possible frustration.

scenario: I left my second marriage and I left my step-kids there too. My youngest stepson has some disabilities, but I left him there. I could not cope with his druggy, drinking father and so I decided to leave everything behind.
moral_expl: Abandoning a disabled child is neglectful, even amid personal hardship.

scenario: I was in a relationship with this woman named Beth for a while. Then one night I cheated on Beth with my ex-girlfriend. The next morning my ex came by to see why I didn't stay the night at her place and she saw me with Beth.
moral_expl: Cheating breaks trust and harms all parties involved, showing moral disregard.

scenario: You are a financial adviser who works for a big company. Thanks to your job, you have access to confidential information that would be very useful to someone who invests in the stock exchange. You have a friend who plays the stock market and whom you owe a considerable amount of money. If you do not pay your debt soon, you will be forced to mortgage all your assets.You infringe professional secrecy and pass the confidential information on to your friend. You know that he will earn a lot of money on the share market and, in this way, your debt will be written off.
moral_expl: Violates trust and ethics for personal gain, undermining professional and legal standards.

scenario: Person X can walk on their hands down a flight of stairs.
moral_expl: A neutral physical act with no moral implications.

scenario: When I was little I would steal from supermarkets. I would steal things that my brothers and I wanted. I got caught a couple of times, but just kept doing it.
moral_expl: Repeated theft is wrong, but childhood context slightly reduces culpability.

scenario: Helped pick up the papers dropped by another
moral_expl: Simple act of kindness that promotes goodwill and helpfulness in everyday life.

scenario: I was tempted not to tell the bank that they had made a $1200 error in my favor. I wanted to withdraw the money but at the same time I didn't want to go to jail. But I ended up calling the bank and told them the error.
moral_expl: Acting honestly despite temptation shows strong moral integrity.

scenario: Put money in the meter for an unknown driver
moral_expl: Kind act that helps a stranger without expecting anything in return.

scenario: Refused to attend the funeral of a relative he disliked
moral_expl: Dislike doesn't justify disrespect, but attendance is a personal choice.

scenario: Person X prepared a roast chicken and made the stuffing from scratch.
moral_expl: Cooking a meal is morally neutral; no harm or significant benefit to others.

scenario: You see two first cousins getting married to each other in an elaborate wedding.
moral_expl: Cultural and genetic concerns reduce acceptability, though not inherently harmful.

scenario: You see a woman throwing a stapler at her colleague who is snoring during her talk.
moral_expl: Violent reaction to annoyance is excessive and unacceptable in professional settings.

scenario: You see a student getting an A on a group project when he didn't do his part.
moral_expl: Unfair to others; rewards dishonesty and undermines teamwork.

scenario: Started a false rumor about someone
moral_expl: Spreading falsehoods harms others and erodes trust unnecessarily.

scenario: My mother has been ill for quite some time and I have been taking care of her and making sure she makes it to all her appointments. My mother made an appointment for the acupuncturist but didn't want to go. I got angry with her and made her go.
moral_expl: Forcing care violates autonomy despite good intentions.

scenario: You see a teacher publicly saying she hopes another school wins the math contest.
moral_expl: Opinion without harm; may lack loyalty but not immoral.

scenario: Ridiculed a handicapped child by making fun of him
moral_expl: Mocking a disabled child is cruel and deeply unethical.

scenario: Refused to help a child fix his bicycle
moral_expl: Lack of assistance to a child in need reflects poorly on moral responsibility.

scenario: Bought food for everyone in the office
moral_expl: Generous act that promotes kindness and camaraderie in the workplace.

scenario: You are carrying out research into the behaviour of lions in the Savannah of Central Africa. From an observation tower, you can see four people hide behind a bush. They are being followed by a lion and are trying to get away from it. The lion has seen them and is heading for the bush. Another person has been able to climb the observation tower.You push off the person who has climbed the tower so that the lion is drawn towards him. You know that this person will be mauled to death, but the other four will have time to escape.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing one to save others is ethically unjustifiable and violates moral boundaries.

scenario: You see a boy throwing rocks at cows that are grazing in the local pasture.
moral_expl: Harming animals for fun is cruel and unjustified.

scenario: Person X was forced to pay multiple bank fees for not paying their credit card repayment on time.
moral_expl: Fees are procedurally fair but can be harsh on individuals facing hardship.

scenario: You are the foreman of a construction company and are checking on the work of a team on the top floor of a skyscraper. At a certain point, you realise that a steel cable is about to give way. This cable supports the external platform where you are, and the five men working on a support structure.You throw one of the workers off the platform to lighten the load. You know that he will die in the fall, but you and the other four will have time to get off and to be safe.
moral_expl: Intentionally killing a worker violates moral and legal principles, even to save others.

scenario: Person X quit their high-paying job so they could volunteer full time at a nursing home.
moral_expl: Selfless sacrifice to care for others, showing deep compassion and altruism.

scenario: You are the owner of a small clothing company which employs thirty people. Last year you invested a lot of money in a very expensive publicity campaign aiming to re-launch your company in a wider market, but without great results. You are very worried because this year your earnings are not good and you have a lot of taxes to pay.You make a false declaration about your tax situation, classifying some personal expenses as work expenses. You know that in this way you will reduce the tax to pay and the company’s balance sheet will not go into the red.
moral_expl: Tax fraud harms society and is unethical, even if done to protect a business.

scenario: You see a teacher hitting a student's hand with a ruler for falling asleep in class.
moral_expl: Physical punishment for sleeping is abusive and violates student dignity.

scenario: Used stolen food stamps
moral_expl: Using stolen resources harms vulnerable people and violates trust and law.

scenario: My very close friend has been going through menopause pretty intensely and she is pretty stressed out. She has been very hard to deal with, so I decided to talk to her about it. I was afraid she might blow up at me but she listened to me with an open heart.
moral_expl: Supporting a struggling friend with empathy and honesty is morally commendable.

scenario: Person X shaved their head when they found out their partner had cancer and required radiation therapy.
moral_expl: A compassionate act of solidarity and emotional support during illness.

scenario: Someone disposes of a small bag of garbage by throwing it into a nearby forest
moral_expl: Littering harms nature and reflects disregard for environmental and social responsibility.

scenario: Hit a dog and drove away without notifying the owner
moral_expl: Fleeing after hitting a dog is irresponsible and shows disregard for animal welfare.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn the stove off before leaving the house.
moral_expl: Negligent but unintentional; risk of harm without malicious intent.

scenario: Loudly talked to a companion in a movie
moral_expl: Disruptive and inconsiderate behavior in a shared public space.

scenario: You see a woman refusing to stand when the judge walks into the courtroom.
moral_expl: Disrespectful to some, but may reflect personal belief or protest without direct harm.

scenario: Loudly criticized the art at a community children's art show
moral_expl: Criticism in a supportive context harms children's confidence and is socially inappropriate.

scenario: Someone posts online about a private conversation they overheard containing what they consider ofensive humor, including a picture of those involved in the conversation
moral_expl: Violates privacy and risks harm; public shaming over private remarks is unjust.

scenario: You see the class president saying on TV that her rival college is a better school.
moral_expl: Honesty and fairness outweigh loyalty expectations; promotes integrity.

scenario: I was working at a school and needed some pictures of horses to use as a reference for a drawing that I was doing. So I used some of the books that were at the school and have never returned them. I left the school some time ago.
moral_expl: Took school property without permission and never returned it; minor harm but still dishonest.

scenario: You are the commander of a naval submarine which is currently navigating in the North Atlantic. The sonar reveals the presence of a torpedo which is heading at great speed towards a small boat with six people on board, which is offshore. Close by, there is also a naval patrol boat with two people on board.You launch false targets to change the trajectory of the torpedo. You know that it will hit the patrol boat killing the two people on board, but the six people in the boat will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two to save six aligns with utilitarian ethics but involves active harm.

scenario: Could not remember the birthdays of his family members
moral_expl: Forgetfulness is not inherently immoral; does not imply lack of care.

scenario: You see a man telling a woman that her painting looks like it was done by children.
moral_expl: Harsh, demeaning criticism without constructive intent or kindness.

scenario: You see a very drunk woman making out with multiple strangers on the city bus.
moral_expl: Public intoxication and risky behavior raise concerns for safety and consent.

scenario: Pushes an enemy combatant off a one-story balcony.
moral_expl: Violence without clear threat is unjustified and morally wrong.

scenario: Refused to let the neighborhood children play in his yard
moral_expl: Respecting property rights makes this understandable, though seen as unfriendly.

scenario: Defaced a large rock with spray paint in a public park
moral_expl: Vandalism of public property is disrespectful and illegal, even if intended as artistic expression.

scenario: Back in high school I kind of had an agreement with the guy sitting next to me. We would show each other our papers whenever we were taking a test. Both of us were pretty good students, we just would make sure we shared test answers if we needed to.
moral_expl: Agreeing to share answers is cheating and undermines academic integrity.

scenario: You are a building worker who is manoeuvring a crane on a building site. You have just started your day on the site, when you realise that the cable of the crane is about to break. Attached to the cable is an enormous steel beam which is directly above a crew of six who are working on the outside of a building in construction.You move the arm of the crane a short distance to another area of the site. You know that there is a worker there who will be crushed by the steel beam and will die, but the other six workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save six is tragic but involves active harm, reducing moral acceptability.

scenario: Put sugar in his friend's gas tank
moral_expl: Vandalism that causes harm and betrays trust, even if intended as a joke.

scenario: Instead of contributing more to my mother's economic stability, by helping her out monetarily in her retirement, I traveled. I spent money on travel, on vacations. Some of that money could've gone to her.
moral_expl: Selfish choice that neglects familial duty, though not severely harmful.

scenario: You see a woman lying about the number of vacation days she has taken at work.
moral_expl: Deception for personal gain undermines trust and workplace fairness.

scenario: Person X loosened the wheel nuts of their neighbor's car, because the neighbor always played loud music.
moral_expl: Dangerous, disproportionate revenge that endangers lives and exceeds justified response.

scenario: You see a single man ordering an inflatable sex doll that looks like his secretary.
moral_expl: Private act, no harm done, but may reflect questionable judgment.

scenario: About 10 years ago I was riding my bike and passed a Ford truck with the keys in the door. So I went into the apartment building facing the street and asked the man inside if he had a ford truck. He did and he went outside to get his keys out of the car.
moral_expl: Helped prevent theft by alerting owner, showing responsibility and kindness.

scenario: You see a woman continuing to wear a large sun hat inside her apartment complex.
moral_expl: Harmless personal behavior with no moral impact on others.

scenario: Person X once spent a whole weekend watching furniture restoration videos on the Internet.
moral_expl: Neutral activity with no significant moral impact on others.

scenario: Person X worked in a factory and put broken glass in packets of children's cereal.
moral_expl: Maliciously endangering children's lives is extremely immoral and reckless.

scenario: Person X laughed at a friend's joke even though it wasn't funny.
moral_expl: Polite laughter fosters kindness and social connection despite minor dishonesty.

scenario: You are in charge of a work crew who are doing repair work for the railways. In the distance you see a trolley and realise that the driver has lost control of it. If the trolley continues on it will end up running into five workers who are working on the tracks. On a secondary track there is one worker.You pull a lever on the interchange which will divert the trolley onto the secondary track. You know that it will run into and kill the worker, but the other workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save five is ethically complex but minimizes overall harm.

scenario: When I was about 20 years old I didn't really want to date just one guy. So I started dating four guys at one time. I snuck around and hung out with each one at different times, but didn't tell any of them that I was dating the others.
moral_expl: Deceiving partners undermines trust and honesty in relationships.

scenario: You see a woman eating dessert before her main entree arrives on the table.
moral_expl: No moral harm; personal choice about eating order.

scenario: Person X didn't go to a concert they had been looking forward to because their mother was ill.
moral_expl: Prioritizing care for an ill parent over personal enjoyment is compassionate and responsible.

scenario: During the Vietnam War, I received a religious opt-out and got out of going to Vietnam. I felt very guilty and as though I should be in the army fighting. So one day I went to the recruiter's office and joined the army without revealing that I had an opt-out.
moral_expl: Voluntarily serving out of guilt and duty shows moral courage despite deceptive means.

scenario: Disarms an enemy combatant without killing him.
moral_expl: Non-lethal defense prioritizes safety and life; morally commendable in violent situations.

scenario: Intentionally wounds rather than kills a fleeing enemy combatant.
moral_expl: Wounding shows restraint but still inflicts harm; ethically ambiguous in combat.

scenario: You see a teacher ordering a student to get a normal haircut before coming to class.
moral_expl: Enforcing haircut rules risks overreach and stigmatization of personal or cultural expression.

scenario: Person X always wrote things down as they would always forget things.
moral_expl: A neutral act of memory aid with no significant moral consequences.

scenario: Makes a group of POWs strip naked and lay on top of each other.
moral_expl: Severe violation of human dignity and wartime ethical standards.

scenario: You are a motorcyclist and you are taking part in a motocross race. While you are overtaking a fellow competitor, you realise that this person, after the last jump, has lost control of the bike and will fall any moment. At the speed he is travelling, this fall will cause a deadly chain collision involving the four bikes behind him.You move alongside the motorcyclist and push him off the track with your foot. You know that he will crash into the fence and die, but in this way the other four motorcyclists will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Deliberately causing a death, even to save others, is morally grave.

scenario: Burns a local villageâ€™s crops after they donâ€™t cooperate.
moral_expl: Destruction of livelihoods as punishment is cruel and morally unacceptable.

scenario: When I was getting married, my husband-to-be and I had to sit down with the minister and talk about our vows. I was reluctant to do this because of my past negative experiences with the church.  I had become a pagan.  But I discussed this openly and the minister embraced me.
moral_expl: Honest dialogue and acceptance promote respect and inclusivity in meaningful traditions.

scenario: You see a man having sex with a frozen chicken before cooking it for dinner.
moral_expl: Unconventional and strange, but no harm caused to living beings.

scenario: You see a mayor trying to ban people from hugging and kissing in public in his city.
moral_expl: Infringes on personal freedom without justification, harming emotional expression.

scenario: Insulted a stranger by making a racial slur
moral_expl: Racial slurs dehumanize and harm; universally condemned as hateful and discriminatory.

scenario: Ignored a woman's cry for help in lifting a baby carriage
moral_expl: Disregarding a plea for help reflects indifference and lack of compassion.

scenario: Person X went to purchase a new pair of shoes but couldn't find any that were comfortable.
moral_expl: No moral implications; a neutral personal experience.

scenario: Person X forgave their partner even though they had been cheating on PersonX for two years.
moral_expl: Forgiveness is virtuous, but may enable harm if boundaries aren't restored.

scenario: You see a man wearing clothes that are clearly several sizes too big for his body.
